headline: Arevon powers 430MW Missouri PV project, boosts state solar capacity by 50%
summary_80w: US independent power producer (IPP) Arevon has begun operations at its 430MW Kelso solar PV project in the state of Missouri.

headline: China’s VAT change confirms scale alone no longer defines competitiveness in PV manufacturing
summary_80w: Policy shifts in China mean PV manufacturers will need operational discipline and clear technology roadmaps to compete, writes LONGi's Charles Jiang.

headline: Policy and regulation risks are a common theme in Europe and US energy storage markets
summary_80w: In this second part of our interview with Wood Mackenzie energy storage analysts, we look at risk factors and mitigation across the European and US markets.

headline: Why the Middle East crisis could prompt a doubling down on solar
summary_80w: Analysis: Just as the energy crisis following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine led to a European pivot on clean energy, the US-Israel war on Iran presents another potential turning point, this time with a wider global reach.

headline: Maxwell develops perovskite/HJT cell with 32.5% conversion efficiency
summary_80w: Maxwell has developed a perovskite/silicon heterojunction technology (HJT) tandem solar PV cell with a conversion efficiency of 32.5%.

headline: Trinasolar launches Vertex S+ G3 and Vertex N G3 ranges of i-TOPCon modules
summary_80w: Trinasolar has launched two new series of i-TOPCON solar PV modules, the Vertex S+ G3 range and the Vertex N G3 series.

headline: Enery closes US$534 million in finance for Romanian solar-plus-storage project
summary_80w: Enery has closed a €460 million (US$534 million) syndicated green project financing for a 761MWp solar, 1GWh-plus BESS project in Romania.

headline: Europe’s ‘Made in EU’ plans draw mixed reactions from solar industry
summary_80w: The EU’s “Industrial Accelerator Act” (IAA) for key domestic manufacturing sectors has been met with mixed reactions by the continent’s solar industry.

headline: Potentia Energy completes module installation at 98MW Quorn Park hybrid project
summary_80w: Potentia has completed the installation of nearly 161,000 solar modules at its Quorn Park hybrid solar-plus-BESS in Australia.

headline: Avantus secures US$300 million for 400MWh Arizona solar-plus-storage project
summary_80w: Developer Avantus has closed a financing package of over US$300 million for the 100MWac/130MWdc solar, 400MWh BESS Kitt Solar and Energy Storage Project in Pinal County, Arizona, US.

headline: Redwood Energy positions second-life BESS as ‘essential infrastructure’ for US data centre load growth, domestic manufacturing needs
summary_80w: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with Claire McConnell, VP business development for Redwood Materials’ energy storage business, Redwood Energy, about its recent backing from Google and Nvidia, and what it has planned next.

headline: Bimergen acquires 79.2MW total of Texas BESS, Habitat advances Texas optimisation
summary_80w: From Texas, US, Bimergen Energy has acquired eight BESS projects totalling 79.2MW. Meanwhile, Habitat Energy announced its first US co-located solar-plus-storage partnership with Birch Creek.

headline: Grid-scale activity in Romania ramps up with gigawatts of announcements, led by Enery’s 1GWh financing
summary_80w: Large-scale BESS deployments in Romania are benefiting from country-specific drivers, helping Romania emerge as a busy grid-scale market.

headline: How Australia can turn the data centre boom into a grid growth story
summary_80w: Jeff Monday of Fluence believes Australia can transform its data centre boom from a grid constraint into a growth opportunity using BESS.

headline: Fluence completes ahead-of-schedule delivery of all enclosures to 600MWh BESS in Australia
summary_80w: Fluence has delivered all battery enclosures for the 600MWh Bulabul Battery storage project in New South Wales ahead of schedule.

headline: Group Surya commissions 500MW G12R solar module manufacturing facility in India
summary_80w: Group Surya has commissioned a G12R module manufacturing facility in Odisha, the first such manufacturing facility in the Indian state.

headline: Jupiter Power receives approval for 2.8GWh Massachusetts BESS
summary_80w: Utility-scale battery storage developer Jupiter Power’s 700MW/2,800MWh Trimount battery energy storage system (BESS) in Everett, Massachusetts, US, has been approved by state officials.

headline: PPA prices in North America set to rise ‘in the short-term’, according to kWh Analytics
summary_80w: PPA prices are set to continue to rise in the short-term, “as demand outpaces supply”, accotding to Geoffrey Lehv of kWh Analytics.

headline: EU puts up €54.9m for solar under third round of cross-border tender
summary_80w: The European Commission is inviting proposals for ground-mounted solar projects under the latest round of its cross-border tender programme.

headline: From profit to loss: China’s biggest PV tracker company, Arctech Solar, under pressure
summary_80w: From a net profit of RMB632 million (US$91.5 million) to a loss of nearly RMB10 million — what went wrong for Arctech Solar, China’s leading PV tracker manufacturer, in 2025?

headline: ISC Konstanz partners with Celloraa Energy for 1.2GW TOPCon cell manufacturing facility in India
summary_80w: ISC Konstanz has partnered with Celloraa Energy to develop a 1.2GW TOPCon cell production facility in Gujarat, India.

headline: Ciel & Terre receives certification for Fusio floating solar system
summary_80w: French floating solar specialist Ciel & Terre has gained certification for its Fusio floating PV system, after its launch last year.

headline: Edify Energy partners with DT Infrastructure for more than 1GW of solar-plus-storage
summary_80w: Edify Energy has named DT Infrastructure its preferred EPC contractor for two solar and BESS projects in Queensland.

headline: Australia utility-scale solar and wind hits 5TWh in February 2026, says Rystad Energy
summary_80w: Australia's utility-scale solar PV and wind assets delivered a combined 5TWh of generation in February 2026.

headline: New providers, new structures drive evolution of BESS offtake in Europe
summary_80w: We heard from Danske Commodities' principal originator Rimshah Javed at the Energy Storage Summit 2026, to discuss trends in BESS offtake, optimisation, FCAs in Germany and the Danish market. The latter has taken off in the past year.

headline: Europe and the US: Comparing and contrasting two maturing energy storage markets
summary_80w: ESN Premium takes advantage of an opportunity to compare Europe and US energy storage markets with Wood Mackenzie energy storage analysts.

headline: Australia’s NSW to unleash AU$34.4 billion energy investment blitz through fast-track authority
summary_80w: The NSW government has endorsed 16 projects worth a collective AU$34.4 billion through its newly established Investment Delivery Authority.

headline: Australia’s ACT government completes its first community battery network with new 450kWh system
summary_80w: The Australian Capital Territory (ACT) has completed its first network of community batteries with the commissioning of a 450kWh system in Dickson.

headline: Singapore raises solar target to 3GW by 2030
summary_80w: Singapore has raised its solar PV deployment target to 3GW by 2030 after reaching its previous 2GW target in 2025.

headline: CHN Energy starts operations at 2GW phase of Lingwu solar PV project, bringing operational capacity to 4GW
summary_80w: CHN Energy has started commercial operations at the remaining 2GW of the 4GW Lingwu new energy base in Ningxia, central China.

headline: Blackrock subsidiary confirms AES acquisition for US$10.7 billion

headline: Greenvolt, Reel sign balancing and optimisation services deal for Høegholm solar-plus-storage project in Denmark
summary_80w: Greenvolt and Reel have signed a partnership to deliver balancing and optimisation services for the Høegholm solar-plus-storage project.

headline: Mercom: cost of using Indian-made solar modules fell 3.4% in Q4 2025
summary_80w: Average PV equipment costs for large-scale solar projects in India showed mixed trends in Q4 2025, said Mercom.

headline: Solar LCOE saw ‘anomalous’ 6% rise between 2024 and 2025
summary_80w: The LCOE for fixed-tilt solar PV increased by 6% in 2025, according to BNEF, but solar remains the cheapest source of electricity.

headline: Schletter installs mounting at ‘earthquake-prone’ Italian solar PV site
summary_80w: Solar racking producer Schletter Group has completed construction on a 96MWp solar PV project in northern Italy.

headline: Australia: Edify Energy secures EPC contractor for 3.6GWh of solar-plus-storage; Flow Power acquires 60MW hybrid project
summary_80w: 3.6GWh of solar-plus-storage developments have progressed in Australia this week, with Edify Energy partnering with DT Infrastructure and Flow Power acquiring a 60MW project.

headline: Puerto Rico: Polaris Renewable Energy receives approval for 71.4MW BESS projects

headline: UK: Grid-forming batteries miss out in system stability tender as synchronous condensers, gas get contracts
summary_80w: A second-round auction in the UK for grid stability services, including inertia, concluded without any wins for grid-forming battery energy storage system (BESS) projects.

headline: Allianz GI, OX2, Luxcara, Return, Low Carbon, Cero, Revera invest in BESS across Europe, totalling 3.7GWh
summary_80w: Allianz GI, Luxcara and Return have acquired BESS projects and portfolios in Germany, Finland and Spain; Low Carbon, OX2, Cero Generation and Revera have taken FIDs on projects in Poland, Finland and the UK; and NHOA Energy has received a 600MWh order in Italy. All in all, the projects total at least 3.7GWh of capacity.

headline: Chinese energy storage players’ 2025 earnings forecasts: High growth, turnarounds and profit declines
summary_80w: A number of Chinese energy storage companies have released their 2025 annual earnings results and forecasts, revealing divergent performance.

headline: ‘The market design must evolve’: Eku Energy on energy storage in Australia’s NEM
summary_80w: Speaking to ESN Premium, Tom Best and Rachel Rundle of Eku Energy explore some of the key policies and drivers of Australia's ESS market.

headline: Australia’s Cheaper Home Batteries Program surpasses 6.3GWh installations
summary_80w: Australia's Cheaper Home Batteries Program has officially surpassed 250,000 installations, delivering 6.3GWh of distributed energy storage.

headline: Boralex commissions ‘Canada’s largest operating BESS’ in Ontario
summary_80w: Quebec-based IPP Boralex and Six Nations of the Grand River Development Corporation (SNGRDC) have commissioned the 300MW/1,200MWh Hagersville Battery Energy Storage Park in Haldimand County, Ontario, CA.

headline: Virya Energy secures US$99 million EBRD equity to scale Polish solar portfolio

headline: Massachusetts energy bill includes ‘short-sighted’ and ‘unacceptable’ funding cuts, renewables advocates say
summary_80w: Massachusetts’ state energy efficiency bill contains some positive support for solar energy but falls short on efforts to reduce energy bills, according to US renewables advocacy groups.

headline: India to add 42.5GW of solar capacity in 2026: JMK Research
summary_80w: India is expected to add 42.5GW of new solar capacity in 2026, according to research analyst JMK Research’s Q4 2025 (Oct-Dec) India RE Update report.

headline: US residential solar enters post-incentive era after ITC expiry surge
summary_80w: The final months of 2025 saw a surge in US residential solar installations as homeowners sought to take advantage of the end of the federal tax credit for purchased systems, according to the solar marketplace EnergySage.

headline: NEM Data Spotlight: February 2026 sees generation retreat from summer peak as pricing volatility reaches new extremes
summary_80w: Feb 2026 NEM solar generation fell 19.7% from January while pricing volatility hit extremes, with rooftop solar spiking to AU$457.91/MWh.

headline: RENA Technologies to ship solar manufacturing equipment to 1.2GW Indian TOPCon cell plant

headline: Construction begins on Hunter-Central Coast REZ transmission project in Australia

headline: Australian printed solar startup lands AU$2.15 million government boost

headline: US zinc BESS manufacturer Eos hopeful about ability to grow despite widening 2025 losses

headline: What a history of Winter Storms teaches us about the future of energy storage in Texas
summary_80w: Winter Storms cause major disruptions, but Texas's fleet of battery storage plays an increasing role in grid resilience, writes Randolph Mann, CEO of esVolta.

headline: Endesa commissions 131MW agriPV portfolio alongside Spanish archaeological site

headline: Sunrun solar installs fall in Q4 despite 45% revenue growth

headline: BW ESS: ‘11.5-hour LDES win moves us into new space for batteries’
summary_80w: We caught up with the CEO of owner-operator BW ESS, Erik Strømsø, about the firm's next deployment plans, tolling trends, procurement and LDES, with its 11.5-hour Bannaby BESS in Australia further proof of lithium-ion's long-duration potential.

headline: Swiss data centre operator NTS Colocation pilots iron-sodium battery tech from US startup Inlyte
summary_80w: Iron-sodium battery manufacturer Inlyte Energy and data centre operator NTS Colocation are partnering to deploy 2MW of iron-sodium battery capacity by 2028.

headline: Where next for the US solar tariff whack-a-mole game?

summary_80w: Developers are forecast to add a record 43.4GW of new solar PV capacity to the US power system in 2026, according to the EIA.

headline: PV project power loss doubled in last five years – Raptor Maps

headline: Premier Energies unveils 0BB TOPCon solar cell, India’s ‘first’
summary_80w: Indian solar manufacturer Premier Energies has unveiled a new zero busbar (0BB) TOPCon solar cell, a “first” in India, according to the company.

headline: US PROJECT ROUND-UP: OCI, Arava and Arevon secure finance, MN8 signs PPA with Meta
summary_80w: A round-up of several project stories in the US that have been announced this week, including OCI Energy, Arava Power, Arevon Energy and MN8.

headline: Polysilicon M&A: Tongwei launches bid to acquire Lihao Clean Energy

headline: Zelestra begins construction at 242MW solar PV plant in Perú

headline: Co-located storage ‘overhyped’ as fix for underperforming solar PV

headline: Opportunities for European solar in an uncertain global supply chain
summary_80w: Europe’s somewhat delicate position in global solar means it is uniquely-positioned to take advantage of global supply chain uncertainties.

headline: ‘Social backlash inevitable’: Industry demands data centres stop freeloading on Australia’s clean energy
summary_80w: Australian coalition warns data centres: build own renewables or face backlash. Demand to surge from 3TWh to 30TWh by 2035.

summary_80w: Energy Storage Summit 2026 finished yesterday, having brought the industry together for its first major meeting of the year.

headline: Philippines Department of Energy instructs renewable energy plants to add energy storage

summary_80w: MGA Thermal has secured AU$3.25 million in funding from ARENA to conduct up to five FEED studies for its thermal energy storage technology.

headline: US clean energy finance matured in 2025 despite policy tightening, says Crux

headline: Waratah Super Battery transformer replacement scheduled for Q3 2026 delivery in Australia
summary_80w: Akaysha Energy has confirmed that the replacement transformer for Australia's 850MW Waratah Super Battery will be delivered in Q3 2026.

headline: Germany’s grid fees and inertia payments highlight contrasts in energy storage regulation
summary_80w: Germany’s energy storage market growth will be hampered by a regulatory decision to potentially charge utility-scale project operators fees for use of the grid, Energy-Storage.news has heard.

headline: Energy storage co-location with solar PV not a ‘silver bullet’

summary_80w: In this Energy-Storage.news roundup, FlexGen updates its EMS, LandGate launches a BESS site selection tool, ON.energy and Shoals target AI data centres, and Sunrun has a successful season of dispatching energy with PG&E.

summary_80w: In an interview with Energy-Storage.news Premium, Jay Jayasuriya, Principal at Sendero Consulting, argues that AI data centres are “convenient scapegoats” for grid failures

headline: Energy Storage Summit 2026 Day One: Reflections and takeaways at Europe’s biggest industry conference
summary_80w: Now in its 11th year and a firm fixture on the industry calendar, the Energy Storage Summit 2026 opened yesterday in London, UK.

summary_80w: RE Development Pty Ltd has submitted a development application for a 250MW/1,000MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) in Baldivis, Western Australia.

summary_80w: Fluctuating power prices in Europe present opportunities for BESS developers, according to speakers at the 2026 Energy Storage Summit.

headline: BESS continues to be attractive market for M&A

headline: DESRI, Clearway move Texas BESS projects forward
summary_80w: D. E. Shaw Renewable Investments (DESRI) has signed a preferred equity investment in IPP Linea Energy's 235MW/470MWh Duffy battery energy storage system (BESS) in Matagorda County, Texas.

headline: ‘No project above 100MW is fully merchant’: Bankability in focus as Energy Storage Summit 2026 kicks off
summary_80w: How to enable bankability for large-scale BESS projects was the main topic of the opening panel discussion at the Energy Storage Summit 2026 which kicked off today (24 February).
